subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, precise communication of topic and sustained focus throughout the report
Well-formulated research question narrowly framed and consistently linked to the analysis
Detailed, replicable methodology with appropriate apparatus and control measures
weaknesses: Methodology section occasionally omits explicit linkage back to the research question
Minor lapses in hypothesis framing and incomplete procedural step
Scale-model limitations and CFD parameter details not acknowledged early
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates sustained clarity of purpose, a narrowly focused research question and a thorough methodology, aligning with the 5â6 band. Minor lapsesâin linking each method step explicitly to the central question, in early framing of model limitations, and in a missing procedural detailâpreclude the full six marks but justify a high score of 5.

strengths: Consistent and mostly accurate use of technical terminology and equations with units and RÂ² values
Coherent application of fluid-mechanics concepts to data, with relevant secondary literature to support analysis
weaknesses: Occasional formatting inconsistencies in equations and significant figures that affect precision
Key simplifications (e.g., incompressible flow, density assumptions) are acknowledged but not rigorously justified
Variable labels reversed in the table and some source data lack proper citation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows good subject knowledge and appropriate application of terminology, matching the upper 3â4 band. Minor lapses in precision, notation consistency and justification of assumptions prevent elevation into the top band, meriting a score of 4.

strengths: Thorough data processing with means, error analyses and regression directly tied to the research question
Logical, coherent argument development linking theory, method, results and conclusion
Identification and discussion of anomalous data point demonstrating strong analytical insight
weaknesses: Critical evaluation remains largely qualitative without quantification of uncertainty impacts
Source reliability is not critically appraised and comparison with external studies lacks numerical detail
Reiteration of how specific trends address the research question could be more explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis and argumentation are solidly within the 7â9 band, showing clear relevance and sophistication. While critical evaluation is good, it stops short of quantifying key uncertainties. An overall score of 8 reflects strong critical thinking with room to deepen evaluation.

strengths: Clear adherence to EE structure with title page, abstract, headings and table of contents
Well-labelled tables and figures that generally support the narrative
Photographs and visual elements annotated effectively to illustrate the setup
weaknesses: Formatting inconsistencies in citation style, figure captions and list indentation
Occasional pixelation and misalignment in images and tables
Mixed use of MLA/APA elements and non-standard styling reduces polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report follows standard structural and visual conventions and supports clear communication, aligning with the 3â4 band. Persistent formatting inconsistencies and stylistic lapses prevent the full four marks, resulting in a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning:
â¢ Technical termsâdrag coefficient, angle of attack, Bernoulli pressure, error relative/absoluteâare employed consistently and usually accurately, evidencing sound understanding.  
â¢ Equations are presented correctly and variables are defined; graphs include units and RÂ² values.  
â¢ Minor lapses (e.g., mixing SpanishâEnglish labels, one instance of âCââ for C_d, occasional lower-case âbernoulliâ) slightly reduce precision, so the strand aligns with the upper end of the 3â4 band, meriting a score of 4 rather than 5â6.

â¢ The argument is developed logically from theory â method â results â conclusion; each section references the previous one, maintaining coherence.  
â¢ Quantitative evidence (tables, graphs, regression equations) is woven into the narrative, and the conclusions explicitly address the hypothesis with reasoned justification.  
â¢ Some reasoning is repetitive and, at times, circular (using assumed C_d values to justify drag results), and the scale-model limitation weakens ultimate claims. Nevertheless, the line of argument remains persuasive and well structured, meriting a score at the bottom of the 7-9 band (7).

â¢ The methodology is detailed and replicable: clear variable tables, control measures (constant air density, fixed flow speed, set distances), five trials per angle, and explicit data-collection procedures.  
â¢ Appropriate apparatus (anemometer, glycerine smoke, grid-fin model, controlled fans) and relevant analytical tools (regression, error propagation) are chosen deliberately to address both drag and pressure aspects.  
â¢ However, reliance on secondary sources for drag-coefficient values and the unavoidable scale reduction limit completeness. While these constraints are acknowledged, they prevent the investigation from demonstrating fully optimal methodological sophistication, so a score of 5 (within the 5â6 band) is warranted rather than the full 6.

â¢ The student applies core fluid-mechanics conceptsâdrag equation, Bernoulliâs principleâand integrates them coherently with experimental findings. Calculations of drag, pressure and error estimates show solid conceptual grasp.  
â¢ Relevant secondary literature (e.g., White 2016; conference papers) is used to justify coefficients and to compare findings, indicating good but not exhaustive command of subject knowledge.  
â¢ Key simplifications (incompressible flow, constant density, neglect of Reynolds number effects) are acknowledged but not rigorously treated, and some numerical assumptions (e.g., area approximation) lack validation. These factors place the work comfortably in the 3â4 band at the upper mark of 4.

â¢ The student identifies methodological weaknesses (scale effects, secondary coefficients, environmental factors) and acknowledges an anomalous data point, suggesting potential causes.  
â¢ However, the evaluation is mainly qualitative; it does not quantify how uncertainties in C_d or compressibility might affect final drag calculations, nor does it propose detailed strategies to mitigate these limitations beyond general suggestions.  
â¢ This demonstrates a âgoodâ but not âexcellentâ level of critical evaluation, aligning with the middle of the 4-6 band; a score of 5 is appropriate.

â¢ The topicâhow the angle of attack of Falcon-9 grid-fins affects drag force and pressure during landingâis expressed explicitly in the title, abstract, and throughout the introduction. The student explains why the issue is scientifically relevant (re-entry stability, space-debris mitigation) and links it convincingly to Physics.  
â¢ Purpose and context are maintained consistently; each major section (theory, method, results, analysis, conclusions) repeatedly relates discussion back to the stated focus. Diagrams and background paragraphs reinforce comprehension for a non-specialist reader.  
â¢ Language is largely clear, precise and purposeful. Minor lapses in concision or occasional L1 interference do not impede communication. Overall, the strand fulfils the descriptor for the top band (5â6) and justifies the upper score of 6 because clarity, relevance and focus are sustained at a consistently high level.

â¢ Tables and graphs are generally well labelled with units, regression equations and error bars; visual aids clearly support the narrative and help readers follow the analysis.  
â¢ Nonetheless, some photographs are pixelated or poorly cropped; a few captions are duplicated or misspelled, and minor misalignments occur in tables. These shortcomings detract from overall visual clarity and consistency.  
â¢ Given that layout remains effective despite imperfections, the strand meets the descriptors for the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects solid but not exemplary execution.

â¢ The research question is stated verbatim on the title page and is narrowly framed around one independent variable (grid-fin angle) and two dependent variables (drag force and pressure) for a single vehicle in a defined flight phase.  
â¢ Subsequent sections (method, data processing, analysis) are explicitly linked to answering that question, demonstrating a strong line of inquiry.  
â¢ Some phrasing in the variables table confuses dependent/independent labels, and the question could have been refined further to incorporate scale limitations. These small clarity issues place the work solidly in the 5â6 band but justify the lower mark (5) rather than the maximum.

â¢ Data are thoroughly processed: multi-trial means, absolute/relative errors, regression analysis and identification of an anomalous point. The student links results directly to the research question, demonstrating clear, relevant analysis.  
â¢ Interpretation considers linear and non-linear trends, provides quantitative gradients and RÂ² values, and connects numerical outcomes to underlying physics (flow restriction, pressure variation).  
â¢ While sophisticated computational fluid dynamics or deeper statistical treatment (e.g., uncertainty propagation in derived quantities) is absent, the overall analytical quality is solidly âgoodâ and begins to show elements of sophistication, aligning with the lower end of the 7-9 band (score 7).

â¢ The essay follows EE structural conventions: title page with word count, abstract/ introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, and full references; section headings and a table of contents aid navigation.  
â¢ Citations and bibliography are present and broadly consistent, though minor formatting inconsistencies (mix of MLA/APA elements) and non-standard accent use (âImÃ¡genâ) reduce polish.  
â¢ Overall structure is clear but occasional redundancy (duplicate image captions, repeated text) affects flow, placing the work at the lower end of the 3-4 band (score 3).

annotations.comment: The drag force formula $$F_D=\tfrac12\rho v^2C_dA$$ is correctly presented and variables are defined immediately thereafter.
The introduction heading is clearly formatted, providing a strong structural start to the section.
The experiment description lacks explicit linkage back to the research question; reiterate how each measured variable answers the central inquiry.
The drag force equation is introduced with comma decimals and HTML tags; convert to consistent LaTeX format $$F_d=\tfrac12C_dA\rho V^2$$ for precision.
The large data table would benefit from splitting by angle group or adding shading for rows to improve readability.
Axes in GrÃ¡fica 1 lack LaTeX formatting for variable names; label axes with $$\theta\,(Â°)$$ and $$v_{out}\,(m/s)$$ to maintain consistency.
Citation style varies in the theory section; ensure uniform formatting (e.g. APA or MLA) across all references.
The title is clear, specific and conveys the focus on grid-fin angle, drag force and pressure during Falcon-9 landing, establishing a precise scope for the investigation.
The research question is narrowly framed around one independent variable (grid-fin angle) and two dependent variables (drag force and pressure), maintaining strong focus.
The word count is provided and is within the 4 000-word limit, fulfilling the formatting requirement.
Indentation of sub-entries (e.g. 'Marco teÃ³rico') is inconsistent; standardize levels to improve visual hierarchy.
The discussion of space-debris mitigation and social impact demonstrates strong subject-knowledge application and relevance.
Figure 1 lacks a proper caption element and figure number formatting; use a standardized caption style below the image.
Page numbering is not visible in the footer, making navigation and reference difficult; add consistent page numbers to each page.
The table of contents is detailed and well organized, aiding reader navigation through sections.
Excessive significant figures are shown in the drag table; round values to reflect the precision of the measurements and apparatus.
Identification and discussion of the 20Â° outlier in GrÃ¡fica 2 demonstrates good critical analysis of anomalous data.
The discussion quantifies variation in drag but does not address whether the observed change is aerodynamically significant for real-world landing stability.
The introduction outlines context well but omits mention of scale-model limitations, which should be acknowledged early to frame experimental constraints.
The methodology overview in the introduction lacks explicit detail on trial count and repetition strategy; include key procedural numbers here for clarity.
Sources are cited but not critically evaluated for reliability; include brief assessment of source credibility and potential bias.
The experiment description does not explicitly refer to the hypothesis; restate the hypothesis here to connect design and expected outcome.
The hypothesis is clearly stated but does not give the predicted mathematical relationship for pressure vs angle; specify expected linear or quadratic trend explicitly.
The photograph of materials is well annotated, providing clear visual support for the experimental setup.
Method steps use an unnumbered list style that conflates item types; adopt consistent numbering or lettering for clarity.
Step âe.â in the procedure is incomplete; clarify the missing action to avoid ambiguity in replication.
The final assembly photograph lacks a scale bar or dimension reference; include this to allow accurate interpretation of model size.
When transitioning from data to analysis, explicitly restate how the trends in GrÃ¡fica 1 and 2 address the original research question.
The bullet list uses HTML list-style none, which reduces readability; adopt standard bullet formatting with indentation and uniform spacing.
The Bernoulli equation is correctly formatted and presented as $$P_1+\tfrac12\rho v_1^2+\rho gh_1=P_2+\tfrac12\rho v_2^2+\rho gh_2$$, with subsequent definitions.
The Bernoulli application omits discussion of compressibility effects at high re-entry speeds; acknowledge this assumption and its implications.
The CFD simulation image lacks specification of boundary conditions and mesh resolution; include key parameters to enhance methodological transparency.
The âExperimentoâ heading is prominently styled, clearly signaling the start of the experimental procedure.
The assumed density of glycerine vapor (3.17 kg/mÂ³) is given without justification; cite the source or experimental measurement method.
In the variables table, âDependientesâ and âIndependienteâ labels are reversed; correct variable roles for clarity.
Comparison with Bandung Institute research is noted, but lacks specific numerical or methodological points; include direct data comparison for stronger validation.
Bibliography mixes citation styles (journals, websites); standardize to one style format consistently (e.g. APA) for polish.
The descriptive explanation of drag lacks concise conceptual summary; consider adding a succinct statement linking drag to Reynolds number and flow regime.
Drag coefficients from secondary sources are used without uncertainties; quantify coefficient uncertainty to assess its impact on final drag values.
The Bernoulli application includes $$\rho gh$$ terms for small height differences; argue why these terms can be neglected or justify their inclusion.
Pressure results are reported with excessive precision; round to reflect measurement uncertainty and improve clarity.
